title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A timeless romance exploring the themes of love, social standing, and misunderstanding.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: A gothic novel about a scientist who creates a sentient creature with tragic consequences.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
description: A chilling tale of the infamous vampire Count Dracula and his attempt to move from Transylvania to England.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
description: A powerful coming-of-age story of an orphaned girl who becomes a governess.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: An epic tale of obsession and revenge between a sea captain and a white whale.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: A collection of mysteries solved by the brilliant detective Sherlock Holmes.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
description: A young man sells his soul to retain youth and beauty, facing moral decay.

title: The Time Machine
author: H.G. Wells
description: A scientist invents a machine that allows him to travel into the distant future.

title: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea
author: Jules Verne
description: An underwater adventure aboard Captain Nemo’s submarine, the Nautilus.

title: The Call of the Wild
author: Jack London
description: A story about a domestic dog reverting to his wild instincts in the Alaskan wilderness.

title: The War of the Worlds
author: H.G. Wells
description: Aliens invade Earth in this early science fiction classic.

title: Les Misérables
author: Victor Hugo
description: A sweeping novel of love, justice, and redemption set during post-revolutionary France.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A psychological drama following a man who believes he can commit a crime without consequences.

title: The Brothers Karamazov
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: A philosophical novel about family, faith, and free will.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Brontë
description: A tragic story of love and revenge on the Yorkshire moors.

title: The Scarlet Letter
author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
description: A tale of sin, guilt, and redemption in Puritan New England.

title: The Jungle Book
author: Rudyard Kipling
description: Stories of a boy raised by wolves in the Indian jungle.

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
description: A tale of betrayal, imprisonment, and revenge.

title: Treasure Island
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
description: A classic pirate adventure involving treasure maps and mutiny.

title: A Tale of Two Cities
author: Charles Dickens
description: A novel set during the French Revolution, exploring justice and sacrifice.
